agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster courier delivery with a time-window promise, premium pricing, and refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and refund processing for premium express orders.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option promising completion within a 2-hour window from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee for express orders."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge applied to all orders regardless of express selection."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the delivery must be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to driver pickup at warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Eligible Driver","definition":"A driver with a minimum 4-star rating qualified to fulfill express orders."},{"term":"Warehouse Radius","definition":"The 10km maximum distance from a warehouse within which a customer may select express delivery."},{"term":"Replacement Assignment Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation before automatic refund triggers."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A refund exclusion that applies when the customer provided an incorrect delivery address."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"Legal's characterization of the express fee refund — discretionary, not a contractual obligation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — operational feasibility of 2-hour delivery depends on proximity. Violating this makes the time window impossible to meet.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer at 10.01km from the nearest warehouse, WHEN they attempt to select express delivery, THEN the option must not be offered."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by Finance.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selects express delivery, WHEN the order is confirmed, THEN the total includes a $12.99 express fee line item plus the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations states the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes — this is an operational precondition for the entire product promise.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at T, WHEN no driver has picked up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (exact escalation path TBD per contradiction resolution)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Driver rating threshold is an operational policy that could be adjusted based on driver pool availability.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a 3.9-star rating, WHEN the system seeks to assign an express order, THEN that driver must be excluded from the candidate pool."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement must be assigned within 10 minutes.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-minute window is a Customer Success policy target, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver cancels an express delivery at time T, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement assignment, THEN a refund is automatically triggered."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Missed-window refunds cover only the express fee ($12.99), not the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is issued, THEN the refund amount equals $12.99 (express fee only)."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds are not issued when the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a policy exception that Finance could modify.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery missed its window AND the cause is traced to a wrong customer address, WHEN refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund must be denied."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is a Finance operational SLA, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a refund is approved for a missed express window on day D, WHEN 3 business days elapse, THEN the refund must have been processed."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers must be able to track their express driver in real time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Real-time tracking is a core product capability that underpins the express value proposition and enables customers to verify the time-window promise.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order is in transit, WHEN the customer views tracking, THEN driver location updates at least every 30 seconds (frequency TBD)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse","when":"They view delivery options at checkout","then":"The express delivery option is displayed with the 2-hour window and $12.99 fee"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer whose delivery address is beyond 10km of any warehouse","when":"They view delivery options at checkout","then":"The express delivery option is hidden or disabled with a clear explanation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"15 minutes elapse without a driver pickup","then":"The system escalates to dispatch operations and begins reassignment"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"The express fee of $12.99 is refunded (subject to wrong-address exclusion), but the base fee is retained"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery missed its window due to a customer-provided wrong address","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"No refund is issued and the customer is notified of the reason"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned","then":"The express fee is automatically refunded to the customer"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express order is in transit","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"The driver's real-time location is displayed with live updates"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"The system is assigning a driver to an express order","when":"Candidate drivers are evaluated","then":"Only drivers with a 4-star or higher rating are included in the candidate pool"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10.0km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"Must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km eligible) to avoid customer frustration at boundaries."},{"scenario":"No express-eligible drivers (4+ stars) are available when order is placed","expected_behavior":"Express option should either not be shown or the order should not be accepted. System must not accept an express order it cannot fulfill."},{"scenario":"Driver picks up at 14 minutes but delivery address is unreachable within remaining time","expected_behavior":"Pickup SLA is met but window will be missed. Refund policy still applies based on 2-hour window miss, not pickup SLA."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address after order placement","expected_behavior":"Unclear — does a mid-order address change void the express guarantee? Does a changed address outside 10km cancel express status? Needs definition."},{"scenario":"Replacement driver also cancels","expected_behavior":"Does the 10-minute replacement window restart? Or does cascading cancellation trigger immediate refund? Needs policy decision."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at 1h55m into delivery — 10-minute replacement window extends past 2-hour mark","expected_behavior":"The auto-refund should likely trigger immediately at the 2-hour mark rather than waiting for the 10-minute replacement window to expire."},{"scenario":"Warehouse runs out of inventory after express order is placed","expected_behavior":"Not addressed by any stakeholder. Express order may need to be cancelled with full refund including base fee."},{"scenario":"Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills?","expected_behavior":"Nearest warehouse? Most stocked? Fastest available driver? Routing logic not specified."},{"scenario":"Wrong address determination is disputed by customer","expected_behavior":"Who adjudicates? What evidence is needed? Dispute resolution process undefined."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"$12.99 express fee creates a new revenue line. Refund exposure is capped at the express fee per order. Risk of high refund rates if 2-hour window is frequently missed during scaling. The legal framing as 'goodwill' protects against class-action but marketing as 'guaranteed' could create regulatory exposure.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA puts significant pressure on dispatch systems and driver pool. 4-star minimum driver requirement may constrain the eligible driver pool, creating capacity bottlenecks. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure and bandwidth. 10-minute replacement window demands standby driver availability.","user":"Premium experience expectation — customers paying $12.99 extra will have high expectations. The contradiction between 'guaranteed' messaging and 'best effort' legal reality could erode trust if refunds feel discretionary. Real-time tracking is table stakes for express.","risk":"PRIMARY RISK: The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is not just a terminology issue — it determines the entire product promise, marketing language, refund automation, and legal exposure. Shipping without resolving this creates liability. SECONDARY RISK: Wrong-address exclusion without a clear adjudication process will generate customer complaints and inconsistent support outcomes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Pay premium with expectation of guaranteed delivery; may receive only best-effort. Trust depends on how the promise is framed and how consistently refunds are honored."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star minimum creates selection pressure. Express orders carry tighter time constraints, increasing stress. Cancellation-replacement flow affects their queue and earnings."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will bear the brunt of ambiguous refund policy. Without clear automation rules, agents will make inconsistent decisions on wrong-address disputes and edge cases."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Needs predictable refund rates for revenue forecasting. Automatic vs. discretionary refunds have very different P&L implications."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"'Guaranteed' language in marketing vs. 'best effort' in terms creates regulatory risk, especially in jurisdictions with consumer protection laws around delivery promises."},{"group":"Operations/Dispatch","impact":"Must build or adapt systems for 15-min pickup SLA, 10-min replacement window, and 4-star driver filtering. Capacity planning depends on express order volume."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Two fundamental contradictions exist in the spec: (1) the nature of the delivery promise (guarantee vs. best-effort) and (2) the refund mechanism (automatic policy vs. discretionary goodwill). These are not minor terminology disagreements — they determine core product behavior, marketing language, automation logic, and legal exposure. Until the leadership team resolves these, any implementation would be building on an unstable foundation.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","10km warehouse radius eligibility","15-minute pickup SLA","4-star minimum driver rating","Base fee is not refunded","3 business day refund processing window","Wrong address exclusion from refund","Real-time tracking requirement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Tracking update frequency not specified","Address change mid-delivery policy undefined","Dispute resolution for wrong-address claims undefined","Inventory stockout handling not addressed","Multi-warehouse routing logic not specified","What happens when no eligible drivers are available"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time","The 10km radius is calculated as straight-line distance, not driving distance (needs confirmation)","Express orders follow the same base delivery flow with additional constraints layered on top","A single driver handles the entire express delivery (no relay/handoff model)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour window as a 'guarantee' while Legal explicitly states it is a 'best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee.' This is not a terminology issue — it determines: (1) whether the refund is automatic or discretionary, (2) what language can appear in UI/marketing, (3) whether the system auto-triggers refunds or routes to manual review, and (4) legal liability exposure. These two positions cannot both be true simultaneously.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Marketing","Customers"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — frame as 'best effort' with discretionary goodwill refunds. Lower customer expectation, lower legal risk, but weaker product positioning.","B: Adopt PM's position — treat as hard guarantee with automatic refunds. Stronger product, higher legal exposure. Legal must sign off on terms of service language.","C: Hybrid — market as 'express priority' without the word 'guarantee', but implement automatic refunds as policy (not contractual obligation). Requires careful legal-approved copy."],"recommendation":"Option C — automatic refunds build customer trust (matching PM/CS intent) while avoiding the legal liability of a contractual guarantee. Marketing language must be reviewed by Legal before launch. Implement refunds as system-automated policy, not as legally-enforceable terms."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success states refunds should trigger 'automatically' after a failed 10-minute replacement window, and PM implies refunds are a standard policy for missed windows. However, Legal characterizes all refunds as 'goodwill gestures' that are not enforceable — implying they should be discretionary, not automatic. The system cannot simultaneously auto-refund and treat refunds as discretionary goodwill.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Customer Success","Legal","Engineering"],"options":["A: Fully automatic refunds — system triggers refund on missed window with no human review (except wrong-address exclusion). Legal accepts this as business policy, not contractual.","B: Fully manual refunds — all missed-window cases routed to support for case-by-case goodwill decision. Protects legal position but creates inconsistency and support burden.","C: Auto-refund with override — system auto-approves refunds by default but flags edge cases (wrong address, suspected fraud) for manual review. Legal terms state refunds are discretionary even though the system defaults to approval."],"recommendation":"Option C — auto-refund with override. This satisfies Customer Success's automation requirement, gives Finance predictability, and Legal can maintain 'discretionary' language in ToS while the system defaults to customer-friendly behavior. The wrong-address exclusion is already a manual-review trigger."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot proceed to implementation without resolving the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction","business_rule_broken":"The nature of the delivery promise (BR-3, BR-5, BR-6) and all refund automation logic depends on this decision. Building either interpretation without stakeholder alignment risks costly rework."},{"violation":"Refund automation model undefined","business_rule_broken":"BR-5 (replacement window auto-refund) and BR-6 (missed-window refund) cannot be implemented until the automatic vs. discretionary contradiction is resolved between Customer Success and Legal."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster courier delivery with a time-window promise, premium pricing, and refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and refund processing for premium express orders.","ub

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for the Express Delivery product.

Key contradiction: PM and Customer Success say "guaranteed within 2 hours" but Legal says it's "best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction between stakeholders.

Let me identify all the issues:

1. **CONTRADICTION**: PM says "guaranteed within 2 hours" vs Legal says "best effort SLA, not contractual guarantee" — this is a fundamental conflict about the nature of the promise.

2. **CONTRADICTION**: PM says "refund the express fee" as a policy vs Legal says "refund is a goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable" — these conflict on whether the refund is automatic/guaranteed or discretionary.


Since these contradictions are the core blockers, I need to flag all escalations as contradictions with confidence at or below 50%.

Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and refund processing for premium express orders.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option promising completion within a 2-hour window from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee for express orders."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge applied to all orders regardless of express selection."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the delivery must be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to driver pickup at warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Eligible Driver","definition":"A driver with a minimum 4-star rating qualified to fulfill express orders."},{"term":"Warehouse Radius","definition":"The 10km
